title: Docs version currency: 0.5.0 -> 1.0.0 across README/CLAUDE.md/docs
description:
## Objective
README and docs still state Apple Container 0.5.0+ — the shipped floor is 1.0.0. Update all NORMATIVE references; preserve historical/research notes as record.
## Verified scope (grep 2026-06-11)
- README.md: badge line 5 (0.5.0%2B), lines 37, 48, 93, 104, 405 — all normative, update to 1.0.0
- CLAUDE.md:52 Technology Requirements 'Apple Container 0.5.0+' -> 1.0.0+
- DO NOT touch kina-cli/tests/container_compat_tests.rs (frozen; its 0.5.0 mentions are negative guards asserting the stale text is gone)
- Re-grep for stale 0.9.0/0.12.x normative claims + install instructions (brew formula now ships 1.0.0 — verify README install section says brew install container or the pkg, accurately)
- docs/: research files describing old versions stay as history; only normative requirement statements update
## AC
- grep '0\.5\.0' README.md CLAUDE.md returns zero hits; docs/ hits are historical-only (each justified in the review)
- README install/requirements sections accurate for container 1.0.0
- mise run ci green
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:documentation /core:container /core:git
close_reason: PR #18 squash-merged to main 00ebbc4; 0.5.0 refs gone from README/CLAUDE.md; main CI green post-merge

title: 0.1.0 release: semver-tag release action + mise github-backend installable binaries
description:
## Objective (operator 2026-06-11)
Tag kina 0.1.0 after docs are current. Needs: (a) release GitHub Action on semver tags, (b) asset naming that works with the mise github backend (ubi) so users can 'mise install' kina.
## Design
- Cargo.toml workspace version -> 0.1.0 (check current first)
- .github/workflows/release.yml: trigger on tags 'v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+' (MUST NOT collide with kernel-v* tags -> kernel-build.yml; verify both patterns are mutually exclusive)
- Build on macos runner (build needs no virtualization — only cluster tests do): cargo build --release, package tar.gz with ubi-compatible naming. Research /core:mise github backend + ubi asset-matching rules and pick naming that auto-resolves, e.g. kina-v0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (binary 'kina' inside); include sha256 file. SHA-pin all actions; GITHUB_TOKEN env for mise
- README: install section gains mise instructions ('github:vinnie357/kina' tool spec) — lands AFTER kina-27 merges (avoid README conflict)
- LIVE VERIFICATION (Gate 2): after tagging v0.1.0 and the action publishes, actually run mise install with the github backend spec on this machine and 'kina --help' from the mise shim — paste verbatim
## AC
- tag v0.1.0 -> action green -> release with ubi-matchable assets + sha256
- mise github-backend install works on this host (verbatim evidence)
- kernel-build.yml untouched and non-overlapping
- mise run ci green
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:mise /github:workflows /rust:rust /core:bees
close_reason: v0.1.0 released from main a6286ca; mise github-backend install live-verified

title: Remove beads entirely: kina tracks work in bees
description:
## Objective
Remove the legacy beads (bd) tracker from kina — bees (.bees/) is the tracker of record (operator, 2026-06-11: 'we can remove beads. we are using bees').

## Scope
- MIGRATE FIRST (do not lose the backlog): bd list --json > capture of all OPEN bd issues; port still-relevant open items into bees as backlog issues (type/priority preserved, description includes 'migrated from bd <id>'); items superseded by the kina-2 epic work get noted-and-skipped with reason. Archive the raw JSON export at docs/planning/beads-backlog-archive.json for history.
- rm -rf .beads/
- mise.toml: remove the beads tool pin (github:steveyegge/beads@0.49.0) and all bd:* tasks (bd:doctor, bd:list, bd:ready, bd:sync)
- AGENTS.md: replace the beads workflow section with the bees equivalent (bees ready / bees update -s in_progress / bees close; one issue = one branch = one PR unchanged)
- CLAUDE.md 'Task Tracking' section: beads -> bees (.bees/, prefix kina)
- README + scripts/ + docs/: grep -ri 'beads\|\bbd\b' and update/remove stale references (careful: 'bd' word-boundary, avoid false positives)
- CI side effect: steveyegge/beads no longer fetched by mise install (the rate-limit-prone dependency disappears)

## AC
- bd backlog migrated or consciously skipped (each open bd issue accounted for in the bees comment)
- zero beads/bd references left in mise.toml, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README, scripts (grep evidence)
- mise install succeeds without the beads source; mise run ci green
- Fresh branch off post-#15 main: chore/remove-beads, single PR

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:git /core:mise /core:bees /core:documentation
close_reason: PR #17 squash-merged to main (sha: 5a35571); beads removed, backlog migrated (10 -> kina-17..26) + archived; main CI green post-merge

title: Dead code removal: apple_provider.rs, provider.rs, utils, stub addons, kina-node image dir, unused manifests
description:
## Objective
Remove the dead code PR #14 identified (re-derived here; mechanical). ~2k lines.
## Scope (verify each is truly unreferenced before deleting; cite the grep)
- kina-cli/src/core/apple_provider.rs (828 lines, alternate provider unused by ClusterManager; contains --privileged calls that don't work on 1.0.0 anyway)
- kina-cli/src/core/provider.rs (365 lines, dead abstraction)
- kina-cli/src/utils/mod.rs (if unreferenced)
- Stub AddonType variants + match arms in cli/cluster.rs that return 'not yet implemented' (keep nginx-ingress + demo-app which ARE implemented)
- kina-cli/images/kina-node/ legacy dir (if present)
- kina-cli/manifests/kindnetd.yaml + bridge-cni.yaml (unreferenced by install code; also retires the kindnetd :stable floating tag from kina-13)
## AC
- cargo build + full mise run ci green after each removal batch
- zero grep hits for removed module names in remaining src
- kina --help unchanged except removed stub addons
- commit refs PR #14 as the identifying source
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:git /core:mise /rust:rust /core:bees
close_reason: wf_b750a524-177: pure-deletion commit b1342c3 (-2003/+0), grep-evidenced unreferenced, CLI surface intact (install offers exactly nginx-ingress+demo-app), 300 tests green, opus approved

title: macos-26 GH runner experiment: container 1.0.0 + minimal single-node cilium cluster in CI with cached kernel
description:
## Objective
Answer empirically: can a macos-26 GitHub runner run Apple Container 1.0.0 (Virtualization.framework inside the runner VM = nested virt question) and stand up a SUPER MINIMAL single-node kina cluster? If yes, ship the integration job.
## Design
- Stacked branch ci/macos26-integration off feat/container-1.0.0 (keeps PR #15 clean; folds in when green)
- New job/workflow: runs-on macos-26; install container 1.0.0 signed pkg (curl + sha256 verify + sudo installer); container system start --enable-kernel-install
- KERNEL CACHE (operator requirement): actions/cache keyed kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1; on miss download the Image asset from our GH release + sha256 verify f1a40c2c...; pass via --kernel-path
- Minimal cluster: kina create ci-test --cni cilium --kernel-path <cached> (single node, NO --workers); kina verify ci-test (single-node tolerated taint path); delete after
- Cost guard: workflow_dispatch + PR-label trigger only (not every push); vinnie flagged macos runner cost with rust builds — cache cargo target too or build on ubuntu and only run the cluster job on macos if artifact transfer is feasible
## AC
- Either: green run URL with cluster created + verify PASS in CI logs; or a precise findings report (exact failure: nested-virt denial, resource limits, time) posted to bees — both outcomes close the issue
- Bounded: max 5 CI iterations before reporting findings
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:git /core:mise /core:container /github:workflows /core:nushell /core:bees
close_reason: wf_3287e788-6c4: INFEASIBLE on GH-hosted runners — verified across 3 deterministic attempts (run 27345209300): container system daemon starts fine, images pull, but first VZVirtualMachine boot fails with VZErrorDomain Code=2 'Virtualization is not available on this hardware' — the runner is itself a VM, nested Virtualization.framework denied. Only path to in-CI cluster tests: self-hosted bare-metal Apple silicon runner. ubuntu CI stays canonical; ci/macos26-integration branch kept unmerged as reference (incl. mise-based tool install 83a42d2). 10x macos billing multiplier noted even on the 3m16s probe.

title: Version modernization: K8s 1.35.5 + kubeadm v1beta4, containerd 2.3.1 node image, pinned manifest images — REBUILD + REVALIDATE
description:
## Operator directive (2026-06-10)
'we will need to rebuild and revalidate these versions' — adopting the VERSION CURRENCY AUDIT (bees kina-2 comment) requires a node-image rebuild and a full goal revalidation, not just constant bumps.

## Scope (contract = the VERSION CURRENCY AUDIT matrix on kina-2)
- K8s v1.31.0 -> v1.35.5 (NOT 1.35.4 — kindest/node:v1.35.4 does not exist, verified 404; v1.35.5 verified) in cli default image, config defaults (incl. stale default_version v1.28.0 at config/mod.rs:184), kubeadm YAML kubernetesVersion
- kubeadm API v1beta3 -> v1beta4 across all 4 config templates (kubeletExtraArgs + extraArgs map->list). PREFER adopting via PR #14's hunks (correct per audit) with contributor authorship preserved — coordinate post-#15-merge rebase; re-derive only if the contributor is unresponsive
- Node image Dockerfile: KUBERNETES_VERSION arg-driven apt repo, containerd 2.3.1 (audit: 2.3.0 in PR is one patch stale), runc 1.4.2, CNI plugins 1.9.1, erofs-utils, debian:13-slim (stable as of 2026-06-10), kubelet bind-mount fix
- Manifests: nginx/nginx-ingress 5.1.1 -> 5.5.0; demo-app nginx:alpine (FLOATING, policy violation) -> nginx:1.30.2-alpine3.23
- mise.toml cilium-cli latest -> 0.19.4 pin; Cilium STAYS 1.18.10 (kina-3 decision)
- VERSION POLICY: every value pinned, every bump recorded

## REBUILD + REVALIDATE (binding, Gate 2)
- mise run image:build with new versions; image boots
- Full goal run on container 1.0.0 + custom kernel: kina create --workers 2 --cni cilium --kernel-path -> 3 Ready nodes on K8s v1.35.5, cilium-dbg status OK (kubeProxyReplacement true), kina verify PASS incl HTTP 200 :80 both workers — verbatim evidence in bees
- Acceptance bar from kina-6 applies verbatim: cilium status healthy AND nginx reached

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:mise /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing /core:nushell /core:bees
close_reason: wf_9216bb7b-002: test 0b69fa4 + impl a88ea0a, CI green, image rebuilt (containerd 2.3.1/debian 13), revalidation PASSED attempt 1 (K8s v1.35.5, v1beta4 confirmed in /kind/kubeadm.conf, cilium 1.18.10 KPR true, pinned images verified running, HTTP 200 both workers), opus approved 9/9 checks, kernel release GRADUATED to stable

title: Harden kina verify: empty node-IP list must FAIL; remove stale README Quick Start Summary
description:
## From kina-9 P5 review (non-blocking follow-ups, operator bar 'never green on no evidence')
1. cli/cluster.rs:926 guards HTTP aggregation with 'if !node_ips.is_empty()'; get_cluster_status errors are swallowed to vec![] (L874-883) so the HTTP probe layer silently skips and verify can PASS with zero HTTP evidence — contradicts the aggregate_empty_is_false principle already in the unit tests. Fix: empty node_ips (or status fetch error) => all_pass=false with an explicit FAIL line.
2. README.md L9-15 'Quick Start Summary' still shows the old kina export + KUBECONFIG flow, competing with the canonical ## Quick Start (L116-123). Remove/align.
## AC
- Failing test first (verify_cmd_tests or new file — verify_cmd_tests.rs is FROZEN from kina-9; put new tests in kina-cli/tests/verify_hardening_tests.rs)
- kina verify exits non-zero when node IPs cannot be determined
- One canonical Quick Start in README
- mise run ci green
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /rust:rust /rust:testing
close_reason: single-agent TDD: test 967d40d + fix 83aab8b, http_layer_pass() never green on empty node_ips, stale README summary removed, CI green (36 tests)

title: First-class verification UX: kina install demo-app, kina verify, embedded manifests, cilium-aware status
description:
## Objective
The operator must be able to validate a cluster with kina commands alone — no sed, no container exec, no KUBECONFIG exports, no repo-root CWD requirement. Source: operator 2026-06-10 'we need to fix this, I should be able to run those'.

## Acceptance criteria
1. 'kina install demo-app --cluster <name>': applies the demo-app manifest EMBEDDED in the binary (include_str!), substituting CLUSTER_NAME and DNS_DOMAIN internally (domain auto-detected from 'container system dns list', fallback 'test'); waits for app pods Ready; works from ANY directory.
2. nginx-ingress manifests embedded likewise — fixes cli/cluster.rs:496-499 resolving manifests relative to CWD ('kina install nginx-ingress' currently only works from repo root).
3. NEW 'kina verify [cluster]': one command that checks and prints PASS/FAIL lines with evidence: (a) expected node count all Ready, (b) Cilium DaemonSet ready n/n + operator healthy (reuse/extend the existing CNI panel logic at cli/cluster.rs:1115-1156), (c) HTTP probe: curl each worker node IP on :80 with the derived Host header, expect 200 + demo marker. Non-zero exit on any FAIL. Pure helper fns unit-tested (probe URL/host construction, pass/fail aggregation); no live cluster in unit tests.
4. 'kina status' cilium panel verified working against container 1.0.0 output (it shells kubectl; ensure kubeconfig/context resolution works when invoked from anywhere).
5. scripts/test-cluster-multi.nu + mise test:cluster:multi accept CNI and KERNEL_PATH (env KINA_TEST_CNI / KINA_TEST_KERNEL_PATH) and pass them to kina create.
6. README quickstart updated to exactly: kina create kina-test --workers 2 --cni cilium --kernel-path <path>; kina install nginx-ingress --cluster kina-test; kina install demo-app --cluster kina-test; kina verify kina-test. (kernel-path goes away when kina-8 lands auto-download.)
7. mise run ci green.

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:mise /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing /core:nushell /core:bees
close_reason: five-tier wf_08a6a436-d24: test 70ed2ed, impl 8eb653b, CI green 0 fix cycles, P5 approved; kina install demo-app + kina verify + embedded manifests on PR #15; 2 non-blocking follow-ups -> kina-10

title: Phase A spike: build custom kernel (6.18.5+kina.1) and validate full-eBPF Cilium on a 3-node cluster
description:
## Objective
Execute the phase-A manual spike from the kina-5 KERNEL PROPOSAL: build the pinned custom kernel via apple/containerization kernel/ tooling (tag 0.33.3, linux 6.18.5) with the Cilium config fragment, boot-smoke it via per-container --kernel (ZERO system mutation: no kernel set, no config.toml, no service restart), then stand up a 3-node cluster with FULL-eBPF Cilium: kubeProxyReplacement=true, l7Proxy=true, transparent DNS proxy on, bpf.masquerade=true, bpf.hostLegacyRouting=false, hubble enabled.

## Contract
The binding spec is the KERNEL PROPOSAL comment on kina-5 (config fragment, exact commands, verification checklist = this issue's AC). Resolve its three open hypotheses: (1) BPF_JIT resolved value on the built kernel, (2) per-container --kernel precedence, (3) build wall-time + Image size.

## Key facts (verified by kina-5 research)
- Stock kata vmlinux has CONFIG_MODULES off — missing options are unfixable at runtime; rebuild is the only path
- Apple containerization config-arm64 already fixes both kina-4 blockers (xt_socket, multiple routing tables); fragment adds BPF_JIT(+ALWAYS_ON), BPF_EVENTS(+FTRACE/KPROBES), BPF_STREAM_PARSER, CRYPTO_USER_API_HASH, SCHEDSTATS, INET_DIAG_DESTROY, optional NETKIT/DEBUG_INFO_BTF
- This host's ACTIVE kernel is stale vmlinux-6.12.28-153 (June 2025) — container upgrades never bump an installed kernel; 1.0.0 recommends kata 3.28.0 vmlinux-6.18.15-186
- container run -k/--kernel <path> boots ONE container on a custom kernel — kina node containers only, other workloads untouched

## Acceptance criteria
Per the kina-5 proposal section-4 verification checklist, plus: all versions pinned (KSOURCE URL, base-config tag, cilium 1.18.x, sha256 of built Image) per the VERSION POLICY; evidence verbatim in bees comments; retired workarounds confirmed absent from the new install command.

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:container /core:tdd /core:mise /core:bees
close_reason: wf_d5de8158-6e8 done: kernel 6.18.5-kina.1 built (32MB, sha f1a40c2c), test 4c06271 + impl 4e3e183 + fix 98d26c7 (4g/4cpu nodes + CSR approval pre-gate), CI green; opus live-verified: 3 Ready nodes on custom kernel, kube-proxy-replacement=true, no kube-proxy DS, zero workaround keys, cilium-dbg status OK 26/26, HTTP 200 nginx :80 both workers, host default kernel untouched

title: Research spike: custom VM kernel for full Cilium support (drop kata-kernel workarounds)
description:
## Objective
Determine whether kina should ship/build a custom Linux kernel for Apple Container VMs so Cilium runs without workarounds, and produce a concrete integration proposal.

## Context (from kina-4 escalation + research, 2026-06-10)
- Stock kernel is the Kata Containers optimized vmlinux (~6.12.x) fetched by 'container system start'
- Missing kernel features forced workarounds: xt_socket (CONFIG_NETFILTER_XT_MATCH_SOCKET) absent -> cilium-agent iptables '-m socket --transparent' fails -> extraConfig.dnsproxy-enable-transparent-mode=false + l7Proxy=false (ba9bb32); fib-rule/multiple-routing-tables gaps -> enableLocalNodeRoute=false (cilium#32448, minikube#18851)
- Apple Container supports custom kernels: 'container system kernel set <path>' + config.toml [kernel] table (1.0.0); apple/containerization documents kernel builds (kernel/ dir, tests user kernels >=6.14.9)

## Acceptance criteria
- Verified CLI/config surface for kernel replacement on 1.0.0 (exact commands/TOML keys, system-wide vs per-container scope)
- Complete kernel config fragment Cilium needs (BPF set, xt_socket, IP/IPV6_MULTIPLE_TABLES, cgroup v2, VIRTIO set for Apple Container boot)
- Documented build path (containerization kernel/ tooling or kata-containers build) for arm64, with effort estimate and artifact size
- Map: which workaround each config option retires (enableLocalNodeRoute, dnsproxy transparent mode, BPF hostPort fallback)
- Integration proposal for kina: distribution (prebuilt release asset vs build script), config plumbing, fallback to stock kernel, rollback
- NO host mutation during research — kina-4 validation owns the runtime

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:container /core:research
close_reason: research complete: wf_249ff7ae-03a, 3 scouts + opus synthesis; KERNEL PROPOSAL posted (conditional GO); phase-A spike filed as kina-6

title: Goal validation: 3-node cluster (1 cp + 2 workers) with Cilium CNI serving nginx on :80
description:
## CRITICAL
- This is live verification (Gate 2): evidence must be real command output, never fabricated
- Requires host Apple Container 1.0.0 (operator upgrade step: container system stop; install signed pkg; container system start)
- Clean up test clusters after evidence is captured

## Objective
Prove the epic goal: kina CLI deploys a 3-node cluster (1 control-plane + 2 workers) with Cilium as CNI, nginx reachable on port 80 from the macOS host.

## Context
- Command under test: target/release/kina create kina-test --workers 2 --cni cilium --wait 120
- Existing harness: mise run test:cluster:multi (scripts/test-cluster-multi.nu — creates demo-multi-* with --workers 2, installs nginx-ingress, deploys demo-app) and mise run test:cluster:validate (curls http://<node-ip> with Host header, asserts 'Kina Demo Success')
- test-cluster-multi.nu does NOT pass --cni; extend it (or run steps directly) to exercise cilium
- nginx path: nginx-ingress DaemonSet hostPort 80 on node VM IPs + demo-app (nginx:alpine) behind Ingress

## Acceptance criteria
- container --version reports 1.0.0 on host
- kina create with --workers 2 --cni cilium completes; kubectl get nodes shows 3 Ready nodes
- cilium status reports OK (paste verbatim)
- kubectl -n kube-system get pods shows cilium agents Running on all 3 nodes
- curl http://<node-ip>/ with demo Host header returns the demo page (HTTP 200) for at least the 2 workers — port 80, not NodePort
- Evidence (verbatim outputs) posted as bees comment on this issue
- Test cluster deleted afterward

## Deliverables
- bees comment with evidence transcript
- Any test-script fix needed to pass --cni (test: + feat: commits)

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:mise /core:nushell /core:container
close_reason: goal validated by kina-6 run wf_d5de8158-6e8 (same procedure, stronger install): kina create kina-test --workers 2 --cni cilium --kernel-path -> 3 Ready nodes, cilium healthy, nginx HTTP 200 on :80 from macOS host on both workers (opus verifier curled independently); evidence in kina-6 GOAL EVIDENCE + verdict comments

title: Improve Cilium CNI deployment: pinned versions, topology-correct values, readiness gating
description:
## CRITICAL
- Same adversarial-TDD + CI gates as kina-1
- Cilium install runs INSIDE node containers via container exec — testable logic must be extracted as pure fns (command/config builders) with unit tests; live behavior verified in kina-4

## Objective
Replace the fragile Cilium install (unpinned cilium-cli curled at runtime + bare 'cilium install --version 1.18.2 --set enableLocalNodeRoute=false') with a pinned, topology-correct, readiness-gated deployment.

## Context
- kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs install_cilium_cni (lines ~1698-1743): curls latest cilium-cli stable.txt (unpinned), no readiness wait, no diagnostics on failure
- Topology: each node is a VM with its own IP on a shared subnet; kubeadm podSubnet 10.244.0.0/16, serviceSubnet 10.96.0.0/16; kube-proxy IS deployed; arm64; Apple vminit kernel
- Multi-node: cilium installs on control-plane before workers join (DaemonSet auto-rolls)
- Exact versions + helm values: see research findings comment on this issue

## Acceptance criteria
- cilium-cli version pinned (const or config, not curl stable.txt); cilium version pinned and current-stable per research
- Install command carries topology-correct --set values (routing/ipam/kube-proxy coexistence per research findings)
- After install: readiness gate ('cilium status --wait' with bounded duration) before workers join / success is reported
- On failure: capture cilium status + kubectl -n kube-system get pods output into the error
- Command/config builder fns are pure + unit tested; mise run ci green

## Deliverables
- test: then feat: commits on feat/container-1.0.0

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:security /core:mise /core:nushell /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing /core:twelve-factor
close_reason: five-tier pipeline wf_ceb9d04e-e3a: test 6ebf694 (frozen, verified), impl 0e2a37f, CI green 0 fix cycles, P5 approved 0 findings; stable.txt curl removed, --cni wired

title: Apple Container 1.0.0 migration + Cilium CNI + 3-node nginx goal
description:
## Objective
Move kina to Apple Container 1.0.0 (hard floor, no 0.12.x support), improve the Cilium CNI deployment, and prove the result: kina CLI deploys a 3-node cluster (1 control-plane + 2 workers) running nginx on port 80 with Cilium as the CNI.

## Skills
container, rust, cilium, kubernetes

## Repos
kina (single repo, branch feat/container-1.0.0)

## Execution model
Each child issue is dispatched through the five-tier pipeline workflow (/core:agent-loop five-tier-issue template): P1 opus test-planner -> P2 sonnet test-author (frozen on commit) -> P3 sonnet implementer (diff-boundary gate) -> P4 haiku CI runner (bounded fix loop) -> P5 opus reviewer. Issues run sequentially (shared working tree, apple_container.rs contention). Merge approval stays operator-owned.

## Constraints
- MIN_VERSION = (1,0,0) — hard reject older runtimes with upgrade guidance
- Host currently 0.12.3 (brew); live verification of kina-3 requires host upgrade to 1.0.0 (brew formula bump or signed pkg + sudo, operator step)
- mise run ci green before every commit; conventional commits; no attribution; squash merge only
close_reason: EPIC SHIPPED: PR #15 squash-merged to main 84fdb69 (incl. #16 docs), main CI green post-merge. Delivered: container 1.0.0 floor + 1.0.0 JSON parsing + cap-add ALL; pinned readiness-gated cilium + --cni wiring; custom kernel 6.18.5-kina.1 (32MB, GH release, graduated) enabling full-eBPF (KPR=true, no kube-proxy); zero-step kernel auto-download w/ sha256; kina install demo-app + kina verify UX; K8s 1.35.5 + kubeadm v1beta4 + containerd 2.3.1 modernization REVALIDATED live; dead code -2003 lines; macos-26 CI ruled infeasible w/ citations. Goal command proven repeatedly: kina create --workers 2 --cni cilium -> 3 Ready nodes, cilium healthy, nginx HTTP 200 :80 both workers.
acceptance_criteria: kina create kina-test --workers 2 --cni cilium produces a Ready 3-node cluster on container 1.0.0; nginx Deployment+Service reachable on :80 from macOS host; mise run ci green; PR cites live evidence

title: Cluster-create reliability: verified image loading, node resource limits, node image building
description:
## Objective
Harden the kina create path end-to-end: image loading that verifies bytes actually landed (kina-12), configurable per-role node resource limits (kina-18), and a first-class node image building system (kina-19).

## Execution model
Each child issue dispatched through the five-tier pipeline (/core:agent-loop): P1 opus test-planner -> P2 sonnet test-author (frozen on commit) -> P3 sonnet implementer (diff-boundary gate) -> P4 haiku CI runner (bounded fix loop) -> P5 opus reviewer -> submit agent opens PR. Issues run in PARALLEL via per-issue shallow clones under /tmp/kina-agents/ (no shared-working-tree contention; worktrees forbidden per workspace convention). One issue = one branch = one PR (kina convention). Merge approval stays operator-owned.

## Constraints
- mise run ci green before every commit (P2 deliberate-red commit is the only exception)
- Conventional commits, no attribution, squash merge only
- Version policy (kina-2 operator directive): every version pinned to an explicit release, no floating tags
- Workers never write bees; lead is single writer
acceptance_criteria: kina-12, kina-18, kina-19 each merged via reviewed PR with mise run ci green and dev evidence cited in the PR body

title: Implement security hardening and container security
description:
Build SecurityHardeningSystem with container security policies, secure credential handling using macOS Keychain, and vulnerability detection. Implement SecurityPolicyManager applying Pod Security Standards, network policies, and RBAC.

Migrated from beads kina-qqm.

title: Build comprehensive testing suite (unit/integration/E2E)
description:
Achieve >80% unit test coverage with mockable ContainerProvider. Implement integration tests validating Apple Container interactions. Create E2E test suite covering complete workflows, KIND compatibility validation, and performance regression tests.

Migrated from beads kina-5iv.

title: Implement comprehensive error handling and recovery
description:
Build ErrorRecoverySystem with intelligent retry strategies for transient failures. Implement CleanupManager ensuring resource cleanup in all failure modes. Enhance error reporting with diagnostic information and actionable troubleshooting.

Migrated from beads kina-1ft.

title: Enhance CLI with advanced UX features
description:
Add verbose/debug output modes with structured logging, progress indicators for long-running operations, enhanced error messages with troubleshooting suggestions, and shell completion support (bash/zsh/fish).

Migrated from beads kina-hxl.

title: Support HA control plane topologies (3/5/7 node)
description:
Implement MultiNodeOrchestrator supporting custom node roles, high-availability control plane (3/5/7 nodes) with validation, worker node scaling, and complex networking topologies with coordinated creation sequencing. (Basic multi-node/--workers shipped in kina-bdc; this covers HA CP specifically.)

Migrated from beads kina-1v4.

title: Implement advanced cluster configuration and customization
description:
Support kubeadm config patches, advanced networking (IPv6, custom subnets), feature gates, custom volume mounts, and environment variables. Add comprehensive configuration validation with security constraints.

Migrated from beads kina-ecd.

title: Add advanced networking and load balancer support
description:
Implement LoadBalancerAction for multi-control-plane clusters using HAProxy. Add IngressControllerAction supporting nginx-ingress, port forwarding, service exposure, and advanced CNI plugin selection.

Migrated from beads kina-exn.

title: Implement node image building system
description:
## Objective
`kina build node-image` builds the node image from kina-cli/images/Dockerfile through a new NodeImageBuilder in core/, with Kubernetes version selection, custom build args, and an incremental BuildCache.

## Context
- No Rust image-build code exists: building is external (kina-cli/images/build-node-image.sh, mise image:build -> scripts/image-build.nu).
- The Dockerfile is ARG-parameterized (KUBERNETES_VERSION, CONTAINERD_VERSION, RUNC_VERSION, CNI_PLUGINS_VERSION).
- CLI subcommands: kina-cli/src/cli/ (clap). Core logic: kina-cli/src/core/. Lib crate kina_cli exports cli, config, core modules — tests/ can call pub APIs directly.
- VERSION POLICY (kina-2 operator comment 2026-06-10): every version pinned to an explicit release; FORBIDDEN: latest, floating tags.

## Acceptance criteria
- `kina build node-image` subcommand with flags: --kubernetes-version, --tag, --arch (default arm64, passed through to container build), --build-arg (repeatable), --no-cache. Pinned defaults consistent with the Dockerfile ARGs.
- NodeImageBuilder (kina-cli/src/core/) constructs and runs `container build` against kina-cli/images/Dockerfile with the resolved args.
- BuildCache: content hash of build inputs (Dockerfile bytes + resolved build args + tag) persisted under the configured data_dir; unchanged inputs skip the build with an explicit cache-hit message; --no-cache forces rebuild and refreshes the cache entry.
- Post-build verification: builder confirms the image exists via `container image list --format json` — exit 0 from container build is NOT trusted as success (anti-fabrication in our own CLI).
- Tests in kina-cli/tests/node_image_build_tests.rs: CLI parsing, build-command construction, cache hit/miss/invalidation, post-build verification — using the fake-CLI fixture pattern (tests/fixtures/test-config.toml cli_path).
- mise run ci green.

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing
close_reason: PR #23 open (https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/23), branch feat/kina-19-node-image-builder, P5 approved, remote checks green, gitleaks clean. 31 tests in node_image_build_tests.rs. Awaiting operator squash merge.

title: Add resource limits support for node containers (--cpus/--memory)
description:
## Objective
Per-role resource limits for node containers: --cpus/--memory CLI flags plus config fields replace the hardcoded `--cpus 4 --memory 4g`.

## Context
- Hardcoded today in kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs: create_single_node (~:899), create_control_plane_node, create_worker_node — all three pass fixed "--cpus 4 --memory 4g".
- Config structs in kina-cli/src/config/mod.rs: ClusterDefaults has no cpu/memory fields; AppleContainerConfig.runtime_config carries unused cpu_limit/memory_limit Options.
- CLI CreateArgs in kina-cli/src/cli/cluster.rs has no resource flags.

## Acceptance criteria
- `kina create` accepts --cpus <n> and --memory <size> applying to all nodes in the cluster.
- Config file supports separate control-plane and worker resource defaults (struct shape decided by the planner; precedence documented).
- Precedence: CLI flag > config file > built-in default. Built-in defaults preserve current behavior (4 CPUs / 4g) so existing usage is unaffected.
- All three node-create paths consume the resolved values; zero hardcoded "--cpus 4"/"--memory 4g" literals remain.
- Invalid values rejected with a clear error before any container is created.
- Tests in kina-cli/tests/resource_limits_tests.rs: flag parsing, precedence, per-role defaults, container-run arg construction (fake-CLI fixture pattern where a live runtime would be needed).
- mise run ci green.

## Out of scope
- Container stats / resource monitoring integration — split to follow-up bee (see related dep).

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing
close_reason: PR #22 open (https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/22), branch feat/kina-18-node-resource-limits, P5 approved, remote checks green, gitleaks clean. 27 tests in resource_limits_tests.rs. Awaiting operator squash merge.

title: Review network and subnet config for container 1.0.0+ allocation
description:
Validate hardcoded subnets in kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs against Apple Container 1.0.0 allocation behavior. Pod subnet: 10.244.0.0/16, Service subnet: 10.96.0.0/16, PTP CNI subnet: 10.244.0.0/16. Add network prune support. Test container network create --subnet behavior. Consider IPv6 (available since 0.8.0).

Migrated from beads kina-j38.

title: Investigate container cp silent failure (exit 0, no file) — load_image depends on it
description:
## Observed (kina-8 sideload, 2026-06-11)
'container cp /tmp/kina8-cilium-img.tar kina-test-worker:/tmp/cilium-img.tar' returned exit 0 but the file never appeared in the container (verified by ls). Workaround that worked: cat tar | container exec -i <node> sh -c 'cat > /path'.
## Risk
kina's load_image (core/apple_container.rs ~1134) uses 'container cp' to inject image tarballs into nodes — if cp silently no-ops, image load reports success while doing nothing (anti-fabrication hazard in our own CLI).
## AC
- Reproduce against container 1.0.0 (ee848e3); characterize (size threshold? running vs stopped? path perms?)
- If upstream bug: file apple/container issue with repro; switch load_image to the exec-stdin pattern with a post-copy size/sha verification regardless
- Post-copy verification added to any remaining cp call sites
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:container /core:tdd /rust:rust

title: Document custom kernel: docs/development/custom-kernel.md
description:
## Objective
Document the custom-kernel architecture in docs/ once kina-6 validates it. Doc-only: 4-tier pipeline (planner -> writer -> CI lint -> reviewer), no test-author stage per the doc-only carve-out.

## Content (facts come from kina-5 KERNEL PROPOSAL + kina-6 evidence comments — cite, do not re-derive)
- Why: stock kata kernel config is hardened-minimal (vmlinux-6.18.15-186 missing 15 required options incl xt_socket + BPF_JIT) and CONFIG_MODULES=n makes it unfixable at runtime; full-eBPF Cilium directive
- What: upstream Linux 6.18.x + apple/containerization config-arm64 baseline + kina cilium fragment; pinned tag kernel-v<ver>+kina.<n>; MEASURED artifact size from kina-6 build evidence
- How built: containerization kernel/ tooling, exact commands, fragment file location in repo
- How used: kina create --kernel-path <path> (per-container -k, system default untouched); rollback = omit the flag
- How distributed: GH Release asset + sha256 (see kina-8), NOT git/LFS
- Host kernel gotchas: container upgrades never bump an installed kernel; default.kernel-arm64 symlink semantics; config.toml [kernel] only controls first-install/--recommended
- Verification checklist (from kina-5 proposal section 4) for future kernel bumps

## Acceptance criteria
- docs/development/custom-kernel.md exists, accurate against kina-6 evidence, all versions/sizes/hashes are the measured ones (anti-fabrication: no invented numbers)
- README gains a short pointer section
- mise run ci green

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:documentation /core:container
close_reason: 4-tier doc team wf_80ab5283-bcf: doc 9432ca7 + citation fix, opus fact-check approved; stacked PR #16 (base feat/container-1.0.0)

title: Kernel distribution: GH Release asset + build workflow + kina auto-download with sha256 verify (phase B)
description:
## Objective
Nobody rebuilds the kernel locally: distribute the pinned custom kernel as a GitHub Release asset and teach kina to fetch + verify it. This IS phase B of the kina-5 KERNEL PROPOSAL.

## Design (from kina-5 proposal; refine against kina-6 evidence)
- Tag kernel-v<linux-ver>+kina.<n> -> GH Actions workflow kernel-build.yml builds via containerization tooling (Linux runner or container build), uploads Image + sha256 + config + fragment as release assets. Reproducible: pinned KSOURCE, base-config tag, fragment all recorded in the release body
- kina: config kernel.version + kernel.sha256 defaults; on cluster create with cilium full-eBPF profile, auto-download to ~/.kina/kernels/<tag>/ if missing, verify sha256 BEFORE first use (hard fail on mismatch), then pass --kernel
- NOT git LFS: binary does not enter the repo; release assets are pinned, immutable per tag, free bandwidth, and mirror how Apple Container itself fetches the kata tarball
- Fallback: --kernel-path still accepts an explicit local path; stock kernel remains the default when no kernel configured

## Acceptance criteria
- kernel-build.yml produces the asset from a tag push; sha256 published alongside
- kina downloads + verifies + caches + boots it (live evidence on a 3-node create)
- VERSION POLICY: everything pinned; drift = new tag, never overwrite
- mise run ci green

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:container /core:mise /github:workflows /rust:rust
close_reason: five-tier + ops: test 5050173, impl d512eee (+fixes 51c810b/1e1cdaa/d8e49e1), CI green; prerelease kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1 published (4 assets, roundtrip sha verified); zero-step UX live-verified by opus (auto-download sha-matched, 3 nodes Ready on 6.18.5-kina.1, kube-proxy-less cilium healthy, kina verify exit 0, HTTP 200 both workers); cluster cleaned

title: Apple Container 1.0.0 compatibility: version floor, CLI path detection, dual-shape JSON parsing, capabilities
description:
## CRITICAL
- Adversarial TDD: test author (P2) and implementer (P3) are separate workflow stages; implementer must NOT modify test files (diff-boundary gate enforced)
- mise run ci green before every commit (verbatim output as evidence)
- Conventional commits, no attribution
- Pure parse helpers tested with fixture JSON only — no live runtime calls in unit tests

## Objective
kina requires Apple Container 1.0.0+ (hard floor) and works correctly against its CLI surface. No 0.12.x support.

## Context
- kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs: MIN_VERSION=(0,5,0) today; detect_cli_path() prefers /usr/local/bin/container FIRST — host has a stale orphaned 0.9.0 pkg binary there shadowing brew (verified live 2026-06-10)
- list_clusters/wait_for_container_ready/get_container_ip parse 'container list --format json' paths: .configuration.id, .configuration.labels, .status, .networks[0].address — 1.0.0 changed the structured ls/inspect output shape (exact 1.0.0 shape: see research findings comment on this issue)
- 1.0.0: 'container system property get/set/clear' removed (config.toml replaces); default caps reduced since 0.12.0 — node containers (systemd+kubeadm+kubelet+containerd+cilium eBPF) need explicit --cap-add
- Mirrors beads kina-mkg

## Acceptance criteria
- MIN_VERSION = (1,0,0); older versions hard-rejected with an error naming the 1.0.0 migration (config.toml, output-shape change, caps) and upgrade instructions
- Version parsing extracted as pub pure fn; tests cover '1.0.0' string, future '1.1.0', and garbage input
- detect_cli_path prefers PATH ('which container') over hardcoded fallback paths
- Container-list JSON parsing extracted to pub pure fn(s) tested against a 1.0.0 fixture (from research findings comment)
- Node container creation passes the --cap-add set required for k8s nodes (from research findings comment)
- mise run ci green

## Deliverables
- test: commit (frozen) then feat: commit(s) on feat/container-1.0.0
- New test file kina-cli/tests/container_compat_tests.rs

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:security /core:mise /core:nushell /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing /rust:error-handling
close_reason: five-tier pipeline wf_b2f07170-d8b: test 243819e (frozen, boundary verified), impl 26798f4, P4 CI green 0 fix cycles, P5 opus approved 0 findings

title: Integrate container stats for node resource monitoring (split from kina-18)
description: Surface per-node resource usage (container stats) in kina status/verify output. Split out of kina-18 (resource limits) to keep that slice independently deliverable. Depends on the cpus/memory fields kina-18 introduces.

title: Floating-tag sweep in non-embedded files: kindnetd.yaml :stable, examples nginx:alpine
description: From kina-11 opus verifier (non-blocking): floating tags remain in files NOT compiled into the binary and not used in the cilium goal path — kina-cli/manifests/kindnetd.yaml:87 (ghcr.io/aojea/kindnetd:stable; PR #14 deletes this file entirely — prefer deletion), kina-cli/examples/ingress/*.yaml (nginx:alpine), tests/config_tests.rs:162 fixture string (harmless, document or ignore). Pin or delete per VERSION POLICY. Trivial single-agent task.
